title: Fix .dockerignore exclude regression
body:
In #1715 we added an additional valid path for `!excludes.Exclusions()`
when considering a full copy of the source directory to the destination
via `ADD`/`COPY`. This breaks the functionality of the `.dockerignore`
file, because `PatternMatcher.Exclusions` is true if any of the defined
patterns contain an exclusion (the pattern starts with `!`). This means
if we have no Exclusions defined at all, the `.dockerignore` behavior
does not work any more.

To still have the optimization in, we now skip whole directories if the
patterns to exclude match.

Introduced by: https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/1715

comments.body: I'm not sure what's wrong with travis but is seems to abort the test runs without any further message...

@nalind @QiWang19 @giuseppe @vrothberg @TomSweeneyRedHat PTAL
This and #1913 are fixing the same problem.  We need to merge one and close the other.  I'd vote going forward with this one.
@giuseppe could you give us a head nod on this please?
@saschagrunert looks like dockerignore tests are failing.

> @saschagrunert looks like dockerignore tests are failing.

I see. To still have the improvement in and let the matching works as for now we would have to extend the information provided by the `fileutils` package. I opened up https://github.com/containers/storage/pull/443 for that.

@saschagrunert thanks for fixing it!  Have you tried if COPY/ADD is much slower after this PR?

> @saschagrunert thanks for fixing it! Have you tried if COPY/ADD is much slower after this PR?

I did not find any larger performance regression (did some local tests) but I think we should prepare an official test case for this.
@saschagrunert do you plan to add a test with this PR, or should we get this in and follow up later?

> @saschagrunert do you plan to add a test with this PR, or should we get this in and follow up later?

I'll prepare something and propose it here on Monday :)

Alright, so the previous vs now COPY results are not really compare-able, since the current functionality is broken on the master branch. In the end we could add a small performance test on top of bats, like this:

```
#!/usr/bin/env bats

load helpers

function measure() {
    START=$(date +%s.%N)
    $@ >/dev/null 2>&1
    END=$(date +%s.%N)
    echo "$END - $START" | bc
}

function checkout() {
    DIR=$(mktemp -d)

    git clone -q "$1" "$DIR"
    pushd "$DIR" >/dev/null
    git checkout -qf "$2"
    popd >/dev/null

    echo "$DIR"
}

function merge_base() {
    git merge-base origin/master $(git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD)
}

@test "copy performance" {
    # Prepare the comparison buildah version
    MERGE_BASE=$(merge_base)
    PREV_BUILDAH_DIR=$(checkout https://github.com/containers/buildah $MERGE_BASE)
    pushd "$PREV_BUILDAH_DIR"
    make buildah
    popd

    # Prepare the test case
    TEST_DIR=$(checkout \
        https://github.com/openshift/cluster-api-provider-aws \
        151ec6cd21b9739206fd16f2ddf9f036318d7917)
    pushd "$TEST_DIR"
    FILE=Containerfile
    printf "FROM scratch\nCOPY . ." > $FILE
    printf ".git\nvendor" > .dockerignore

    # Run the test and compare the results
    PRE=$(measure "$PREV_BUILDAH_DIR"/buildah bud -f $FILE)
    NOW=$(measure run_buildah bud -f $FILE)

    # A diff of 5 seconds will let the test fail
    if (($(echo "$NOW - $PRE > 10" | bc -l))); then
        echo "Copy benchmark results differ too much"
        echo "Previous ($MERGE_BASE): $PRE"
        echo "Now: $NOW"
        exit 1
    fi
    popd
    rm -rf $TEST_DIR $PREV_BUILDAH_DIR
}
```

I tried it out and it works, but I'm not sure if we should add things like that on top of hardware where we have no influence on.
If we want to move forward here then we would have to get https://github.com/containers/storage/pull/446 in first.

@saschagrunert I'm kinda of leery about adding the timing test like you have it.  As noted, different hardware could cause failure just due to bad timing.  

As your testing hasn't found any inherent slowness due to the change, I'm fine with moving forward with containers/storage#446 and this PR.   @giuseppe @rhatdan @vrothberg @nalind WDYT?
@TomSweeneyRedHat, I dropped final comments on  containers/storage#446. It would be good to have more pairs of eyes on it.
